zh.lifeSummary: 當William Everard於1535年11月21日出生在威爾士凱爾納方史爾時，他的父親John Everard26歲，母親Rose Greenleafe26歲。他於1559年5月30日與Elisabeth Squire在英國英格蘭素福爾可霍爾布魯克結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1613年2月25日在英國素福爾可霍爾布魯克享年77歲，埋葬在英國素福爾可霍爾布魯克。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William Everard于1535年11月21日出生在威尔士凱爾納方史爾时，他的父亲John Everard26岁，母亲Rose Greenleafe26岁。他于1559年5月30日与Elisabeth Squire在英国英格兰素福爾可霍爾布魯克结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1613年2月25日在英国素福爾可霍爾布魯克享年77岁，埋葬在英国素福爾可霍爾布魯克。

nameMeanings.name: Everard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Everett  .
French (northern), Walloon, and Dutch: variant of French and Walloon   Evrard  , Dutch   Everhard  .
Americanized form of German   Eberhardt   or   Everhardt  .

variants.name: Everett
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English personal names Everard and Everet. These might occasionally be from the Old English form of the name, Eoforheard (from eofor ‘boar’ + heard ‘hardy, brave’), but many bearers of this name were undoubtedly Normans, who took it from the cognate ancient Germanic Eburhard, Everhard.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: When William Everard was born on 21 November 1535, in Caernarfonshire, Wales, his father, John Everard, was 26 and his mother, Rose Greenleafe, was 26. He married Elisabeth Squire on 30 May 1559, in Holbrook, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 25 February 1613, in Holbrook, Suffolk, England, at the age of 77, and was buried in Holbrook, Suffolk, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William Everard est né le 21 novembre 1535, à Caernarfonshire, Galles, son père, John Everard, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Rose Greenleafe, avait 26 ans. Il a épousé Elisabeth Squire le 30 mai 1559, à Holbrook, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 25 février 1613, à Holbrook, Suffolk, Angleterre, à l’âge de 77 ans, et a été enterré à Holbrook, Suffolk, Angleterre.

ko.lifeSummary: William Everard(이)가 1535년 11월 21일 웨일스 카이른아르폰실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Everard(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Rose Greenleafe(은)는 26세였다. 그는 1559년 5월 30일 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 홀브룩에서 Elisabeth Squire(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1613년 2월 25일 영국 수폴크 홀브룩에서 77세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 수폴크 홀브룩에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William Everard nasceu em 21 de novembro de 1535, em Caernarfonshire, Gales, seu pai, John Everard, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Rose Greenleafe, tinha 26 anos. Ele casou-se com Elisabeth Squire em 30 de maio de 1559, em Holbrook, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 25 de fevereiro de 1613, em Holbrook, Suffolk, Inglaterra, com 77 anos, e foi sepultado em Holbrook, Suffolk, Inglaterra.

ru.South Elmham Saint Cross,Suffolk,England: ст кросс соут элмхам, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Everard родился 21 ноября 1535 г. в местности каирнарфоншир, Уэльс, его отцу по имени John Everard было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Rose Greenleafe, 26. Он женился на женщине по имени Elisabeth Squire 30 мая 1559 г. в местности холбрук, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 3 дочери. Он умер 25 февраля 1613 г. в местности холбрук, суффолк, Англия в возрасте 77 лет и был похоронен в местности холбрук, суффолк, Англия.
